mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 10.2% as of 2020-07-15 reflects pandemic labor shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE increases month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) directly reflects consumer demand strength and forward earnings visibility. Months with RSAFS growth above the 3-month moving average signal sustained consumption momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 535982M as of 2020-07-15 reflects post-lockdown recovery
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following RSAFS growth signals are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25% and remains there for 2+ consecutive months, the zero lower bound constraint signals maximum monetary accommodation. This regime produces positive equity returns as investors rotate into risk assets seeking yield, driving SPX gains over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2020-07-15 confirms zero lower bound regime
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in zero lower bound regime are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.2% or t-test p > 0.15), or mechanism fails to predict directional moves in > 52% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) combined with rising employment (PAYEMS) signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, equity valuations expand due to improved profit margins, producing positive 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.17 and PAYEMS at 139582K as of 2020-07-15 show post-lockdown recovery
failure_condition: Joint positive moves in INDPRO and PAYEMS do not produce significantly higher 20-day returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1000K units signals weakening real estate demand and reduced forward construction employment. This contraction in housing demand predicts negative equity returns over 60 trading days as it signals broader demand weakness.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1186K as of 2020-06-15 remains above trigger threshold
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following HOUST drops below 1000K are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or mechanism produces false signals in > 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0% creates a negative real rate environment. This regime compresses bond yields and forces equity risk premium compression, producing negative 20-day returns as investors reassess inflation-adjusted returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 258.72 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2020-07-15
regime_hint: negative real rate regime with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: 20-day returns in negative real rate regime are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.12), or the interaction term between inflation and rates is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows the broadest demand signal. When annualized quarterly growth exceeds 2.5%, equity valuations expand due to earnings growth visibility, producing positive 60-day returns. Conversely, growth below 1.0% signals demand weakness and negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17205.82B as of 2020-05-15 reflects Q2 pandemic contraction
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following strong GDP growth (>2.5% annualized) are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or mechanism fails directional prediction in > 48% of triggered instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market that raises wage pressures and corporate cost structures. When both conditions occur simultaneously, equity returns compress over 5 trading days as profit margin expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month AND UNRATE decreases month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 139582K and UNRATE at 10.2% as of 2020-07-15 show divergent labor signals
failure_condition: 5-day returns following simultaneous PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.15), or the joint interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.20) in rolling 2-year windows.
